title: Iran declares blockade of Strait of Hormuz in response to Israeli violations
summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps announces the closure of the strait, accusing Israel of violating the ceasefire with Hezbollah.

title: Japan's emperor and empress arrive in Belgium for official visit
summary: Naruhito and Masako begin their visit to Belgium, welcomed by Princess Elisabeth at the airport.

title: Mutilated body of a man found inside a freezer in Kobe
summary: Police are investigating the discovery of an adult male corpse in the freezer of an apartment in Kobe city.

title: Shohei Ohtani announces birth of his second child
summary: The Los Angeles Dodgers player shares the birth of his second child on social media, generating reactions in the sports world.

title: Japanese national team prepares for crucial match against Tunisia in World Cup
summary: Japan faces Tunisia in their second group match, needing a win to advance, while analysts highlight the Tunisian defense.

title: Man stabbed in Mitsuzawa Park in Yokohama
summary: A man is injured after being stabbed in Mitsuzawa Park; the assailant flees the scene.

title: Fire at Tokyo elementary school leaves eleven injured
summary: A fire at an elementary school in Kita district forces students to evacuate and causes 11 injuries; the cause is under investigation.

title: The war in Ukraine and its repercussions in Europe and Russia
summary: Multiple reports address the impact of the war in Ukraine, from the reconstruction conference to fuel panic in Moscow.

title: The impact of artificial intelligence on employment and society
summary: AI generates wage premiums for those who master it, but also poses challenges in personnel selection and healthcare.

title: Prominent figures of the Japanese national soccer team
summary: Several articles analyze the life and career of players such as Ritsu Doan, Daizen Maeda, and Kazu Miura in the context of the World Cup.

title: Netherlands beats Sweden in World Cup soccer
summary: The Dutch national team achieves a decisive victory over Sweden in Group F.

title: Pitcher Sumida achieves his second complete game of the season
summary: The Seibu Lions pitcher throws a complete game with eight strikeouts to seal the victory.

title: Outfielder Asano hits decisive home run for the Giants
summary: Shogo Asano, recently promoted to the first team, hits a home run that decides the game for the Yomiuri Giants.

title: Yubari commemorates 20 years since its bankruptcy and advances in reconstruction
summary: The Japanese city of Yubari celebrates the end of salary cuts and the simplification of procedures with the central government.

title: Chinese travel agencies suspend sale of tours to Japan
summary: After temporarily resuming group travel to Japan, several Chinese agencies suspend sales due to possible government pressure.

title: Labor market for high school students reaches a peak
summary: Japanese companies offer starting salaries of up to 300,000 yen to high school graduates in a very favorable labor market.

title: Start of rainy season in northern Japan with risk of storms
summary: The Tohoku and Hokuriku regions enter the rainy season, with alerts for heavy rainfall and possible thunderstorms.

title: Advances in autonomous driving: new standards and applications
summary: The UN establishes the first international standard for Level 4 autonomous driving, while companies compete in the driverless taxi market.

title: Donald Trump at the center of media attention
summary: Trump appears in several news items: his opinion on world leaders, his new presidential plane, and his influence on Colombian elections.

title: Trade tensions between the European Union and China
summary: The EU plans to tighten restrictions on Chinese imports, while China reaffirms its claim of jurisdiction over waters near Taiwan.
